description: A documented resolution proposal for a Discrepancy that precedes the lifecycle transition and carries the user approval (SDC visibility concept 2026-06-11). Core idea: a Discrepancy is symmetric evidence — either reality diverges from the specification (target=fix_reality) or the specification from reality (target=fix_charter). fix_charter proposals carry the concrete diff of the constraint block; their approval is the osf-schemas PR generated from it (SSOT-as-Git: PR review = audited four-eyes approval, CI validates, gen-flow rolls it out) — the engine builds NO governance of its own for this. Producers are human (steward form), AutoResolveRule or agent (v7 Intelligence, advisory-only — an agent proposes but never transitions itself). Four-eyes invariant: decided_by MUST != proposed_by (enforced by the engine, not expressible by the schema). The Discrepancy transition references the proposal_id so that the approval appears as 'User X approved proposal Y from Z' in the audit chain. The store owner is the discrepancy-engine (system of record).

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: What the proposal changes: fix_reality = action on machine/process/data, the specification stays; fix_charter = the specification itself is wrong and is adjusted as a profile change in osf-schemas

attributes.name: proposed_mode
attributes.description: Lifecycle closure that the proposal implies for the Discrepancy. fix_charter proposals imply superseded (the episode is overtaken by the rule change, linked via resolved_by_rule_change to the osf-schemas commit)

attributes.name: rationale
attributes.description: Rationale for the proposal — for fix_charter typically the recurrence evidence (N episodes without a real action)

attributes.name: proposed_by_kind
attributes.description: Producer class: human = steward form, rule = AutoResolveRule, agent = v7 Intelligence discussion (advisory-only)

attributes.name: proposed_by
attributes.description: Concrete producer: user id, rule_id or agent id

attributes.name: confidence
attributes.description: Optional 0..1 confidence of the producer (especially for agent proposals)

attributes.name: evidence_refs
attributes.description: Serialized JSON array of evidence references: discrepancy_ids of the recurrence history, audit_refs, KG node refs, query results

attributes.name: constraint_diff
attributes.description: Only target=fix_charter: serialized JSON {profileId, constraintId, before, after} — after=null means delete the rule. Becomes 1:1 the content of the osf-schemas PR

attributes.name: charter_pr_url
attributes.description: Only target=fix_charter: URL of the generated osf-schemas PR once opened — the PR IS the approval instance

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: proposed -> approved | rejected | withdrawn. For fix_reality, approved triggers the Discrepancy transition with a proposal_id reference; for fix_charter, approved is only set once the osf-schemas PR is merged (PR merge = approval)

attributes.name: decided_by
attributes.description: Who approved/rejected. Four-eyes invariant: MUST != proposed_by (enforced by the engine); for fix_charter, the PR merger

attributes.name: decision_note
attributes.description: Rationale for the decision (especially for rejected)

attributes.name: audit_ref
attributes.description: Hash-chain ref (vault-hashchain) for the proposal event — same Ed25519 chain as the Discrepancy lifecycle events

relationships.description: The Discrepancy this proposal would resolve
Only proposed_by_kind=rule: the AutoResolveRule that produced the proposal
